records.ety: [Pref. im- in + plant: cf. F. implanter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Implanted; p. pr. & vb. n. Implanting.]

defs: To plant, or infix, for the purpose of growth; to fix deeply; to instill; to inculate; to introduce; as, to implant the seeds of virtue, or the principles of knowledge, in the minds of youth.
quotes: Minds well implanted with solid . . . breeding.
